title: Near Capture
storyPurpose: Meridian nearly captures Spark as missing component.

panels.description: Restraint field from Seris Vale: Meridian commander, sharp features, storm-dark field coat with three-arc sigil, ancient control core gauntlet, artificial Rift amplifier crystal, Guardian override codes's control core snares Spark; cyan leash toward Engine Alpha intake.

bubbles.text: He chooses! He is not a component!
bubbles.speaker: Mira Eggwarden

panels.description: Cael Vesper the Lanternmaster: mid-40s, warm brown skin, silver-threaded dark hair, deep blue and ember-gold coat, brass lantern clasps, half-mask of painted wood; subdued posture — supervised redemption, not fully trusted burns a lantern flare into the field projector; guilt and courage mixed; Nira cuts a conduit.

bubbles.text: I opened doors for them once. Not this one.
bubbles.speaker: Cael Vesper

bubbles.text: Then cut the wire — don't write a speech!
bubbles.speaker: Nira Quill

panels.description: Spark breaks free mid-air; egg wrap blazing; Arkan reaching toward Reactor kill-switch with prosthetic hand.

bubbles.text: Choice is a kind of armor. Meridian never forged that.

dialogue.text: He chooses! He is not a component!
dialogue.speaker: Mira Eggwarden

dialogue.text: I opened doors for them once. Not this one.
dialogue.speaker: Cael Vesper

dialogue.text: Then cut the wire — don't write a speech!
dialogue.speaker: Nira Quill

captions.text: Choice is a kind of armor. Meridian never forged that.

grokPrompt: Original high-energy Western fantasy comic storytelling with dynamic panel composition, dramatic inked linework, richly painted colors, expressive character acting, and clear cinematic action. Original Riftwilds IP only. Black steel and white stone first; cyan plasma, orange molten channels, restrained purple Rift energy accents, massive crystal columns, ancient mechanical architecture. NO purple neon AI-fantasy default. NO Marvel/DC/Pokémon characters or logos. Riftwilds comic "The Forge of Rifts" Issue #8, STORY PAGE 17/25 — Near Capture. Story purpose: Meridian nearly captures Spark as missing component. Layout: three-stack. 3 panels with clear inked gutters. Panel 1 (p17a): Restraint field from Seris Vale: Meridian commander, sharp features, storm-dark field coat with three-arc sigil, ancient control core gauntlet, artificial Rift amplifier crystal, Guardian override codes's control core snares Spark; cyan leash toward Engine Alpha intake. Panel 2 (p17b): Cael Vesper the Lanternmaster: mid-40s, warm brown skin, silver-threaded dark hair, deep blue and ember-gold coat, brass lantern clasps, half-mask of painted wood; subdued posture — supervised redemption, not fully trusted burns a lantern flare into the field projector; guilt and courage mixed; Nira cuts a conduit. Panel 3 (p17c): Spark breaks free mid-air; egg wrap blazing; Arkan reaching toward Reactor kill-switch with prosthetic hand. Characters: mira-eggwarden, seris-vale, cael-vesper, nira-quill, arkan. Creatures: spark. Spark design lock: Spark the Glowpup-line Riftborn hatchling: soft luminous fur, cyan-gold rift markings, large expressive eyes, small crystal growths, glowing-tip emotional tail, steadier aura when bonded; cute but original — not a franchise mascot. Keeper lock: Mira Eggwarden: young adult Hatchery mentor/Keeper, warm brown skin, dark hair in practical braid, travel-stained hatchery coat over Compact robes, Compact lantern charm on satchel, determined protective eyes. Arkan lock: Chief Engineer Arkan: elderly former Riftwright engineer, mechanical prosthetics, ancient engineer robes, crystal monocle, tool harness, burned hands, blue energy veins under translucent skin, regretful brilliant eyes. Axiom lock: Axiom: small floating crystalline fox prototype companion, transparent body with glowing internal circuitry, holographic tail, broken crystal horns, soft cyan-gold light, curious restorative presence. Environment: Forge of Rifts underground civilization. Time: timeless subterranean. Weather: plasma haze and molten heat. Lighting: cyan plasma, orange molten channels, crystal column glow. Continuity: {"location": "Engine Alpha intake", "spark": "nearly-captured-then-freed", "cael": "earns-inch"} Leave empty balloon-safe and narration-safe negative space in upper/lower panel corners. NO readable text of any kind in the artwork. NO Riftwright face.
negativePrompt: readable dialogue text, captions, logos, watermarks, page numbers, UI chrome, Marvel, DC, Pokémon, manga screentone trademarks, extra limbs, duplicate characters, missing companions, purple neon fantasy default, photoreal modern clothing, Pikachu lookalike, Cal Reed, Voltkit, Riftwright face reveal, named saboteur identity
letteringInstructions: Standard speech + narration; keep tails off faces and Spark's eyes; Arkan speech weary/brilliant; Axiom soft creature chirps.
artAlt: Near Capture — The Forge of Rifts page 17
transcript: SHOUT (Mira Eggwarden): He chooses! He is not a component!
SPEECH (Cael Vesper): I opened doors for them once. Not this one.
SPEECH (Nira Quill): Then cut the wire — don't write a speech!
NARRATION: Choice is a kind of armor. Meridian never forged that.
